openmeter/apiconverter.purpose: Thin translation layer that converts v1 API filter/pagination types (api.Filter*) into internal pkg/filter and pkg/pagination/v2 types. All converter functions are Goverter-generated variables; manual code is limited to cursor decoding and the goverter directive file.

patterns.name: Goverter variable declarations in filter.go
patterns.description: Converter functions are declared as package-level var of function type with goverter directive comments. Never implement conversion logic by hand — add a new var entry with the appropriate goverter:ignore annotations and let `go generate` produce filter.gen.go.

patterns.name: goverter:ignore for API-internal type mismatches
patterns.description: When the internal filter type has fields not present in the v1 API type (e.g. Exists, Contains, Ncontains), add a `// goverter:ignore <Field>` directive above the var declaration. Remove the ignore when the v1 API grows the field.
patterns.example:
// goverter:ignore Exists Contains Ncontains
var ConvertString func(api.FilterString) filter.FilterString

patterns.name: Nil-safe pointer wrappers
patterns.description: For every converter there is a *Ptr variant (e.g. ConvertStringPtr) that handles nil input. Always expose both forms so callers never need nil-checks inline.

patterns.name: Cursor conversion delegates to pagination.DecodeCursor
patterns.description: ConvertCursor and ConvertCursorPtr in cursor.go are hand-written thin wrappers over pagination.DecodeCursor. Keep cursor logic here, not in httpdriver packages.
patterns.example: func ConvertCursor(s api.CursorPaginationCursor) (*pagination.Cursor, error) { return pagination.DecodeCursor(s) }

key_file_guides.role: Source of truth for all converter declarations; contains only var declarations and goverter directives. Run `go generate ./` here to regenerate filter.gen.go.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding hand-written conversion logic here — all logic must live in the generated file.

key_file_guides.role: Goverter-generated converter implementations. DO NOT EDIT — header says `Code generated by github.com/jmattheis/goverter, DO NOT EDIT.`
key_file_guides.watch_for: Manual edits; they will be overwritten on next `go generate`.
Duplicating cursor decoding logic elsewhere in httpdriver packages.

anti_patterns: Hand-writing conversion logic in filter.go instead of declaring a goverter var and regenerating
Editing filter.gen.go directly — it is fully generated
Adding domain business logic here; this package is a pure type-translation boundary
Forgetting to add goverter:ignore for fields that exist on internal types but not on the v1 API type
Adding v3 API types here — v3 has its own converter layer

decisions.decision: Use Goverter variable-style converters instead of methods or standalone functions
decisions.rationale: Variable-style converters allow recursive references (e.g. ConvertString calling itself for And/Or slices) and are initialized in a single init() block, keeping the generated code self-contained.

decisions.decision: Keep cursor conversion hand-written rather than Goverter-generated
decisions.rationale: pagination.DecodeCursor performs base64 decoding and cannot be mechanically derived by field mapping, so it must stay hand-written.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new filter type converter for a hypothetical FilterEnum API type

openmeter/session.purpose: Provides context-scoped authentication session storage and retrieval for the cloud/managed-hosting auth layer. Stores AuthenticationSession (org ID, role, permissions, user ID) in context under a typed key; used by auth middleware to propagate identity through request handlers.

patterns.name: Typed context key to avoid collisions
patterns.description: AuthenticatorContextKey is a named string type; the constant AuthenticationSessionKey is of that type. Never use a plain string as a context key in this package.
patterns.example:
type AuthenticatorContextKey string
const AuthenticationSessionKey AuthenticatorContextKey = "active_organization_id"

patterns.name: GetActiveSession returns nil on missing/wrong type
patterns.description: Use a type assertion with ok-check (`ctx.Value(key).(*AuthenticationSession)`) and return nil instead of panicking. Callers must nil-check before using the session.
patterns.example:
if c, ok := ctx.Value(AuthenticationSessionKey).(*AuthenticationSession); ok { return c }
return nil

patterns.name: Validate on construction
patterns.description: NewAuthenticationSession calls session.Validate() before returning; invalid sessions are rejected at construction time, not at read time.
patterns.example:
session, err := NewAuthenticationSession(orgID, orgSlug, orgRole, userID, perms)
// err non-nil if OrgID empty or both OrgRole and OrgPermissions empty

patterns.name: WithLogger enriches slog with session fields
patterns.description: Use AuthenticationSession.WithLogger(logger) to add orgId, userId, orgSlug, orgRole, orgPermissions as structured log fields instead of repeating slog.String(...) calls.

key_file_guides.role: Single file containing the entire package: context key, AuthenticationSession struct, constructor, validator, context getter helpers, and logger enrichment.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Storing the session by value (not pointer) in context — the getter type-asserts to *AuthenticationSession, so storing a value type will always return nil.

anti_patterns: Storing AuthenticationSession by value in context (must be pointer *AuthenticationSession)
Using a plain string context key instead of the typed AuthenticatorContextKey constant
Calling ctx.Value(AuthenticationSessionKey) outside of GetActiveSession — always use the helper
Adding business logic (authorization checks, permission resolution) to this package — it is purely a session carrier

decisions.decision: OrgRole OR OrgPermissions must be non-empty, not both required
decisions.rationale: Different auth flows supply either a role string or a fine-grained permission list; requiring only one of them keeps the session compatible with both.

openmeter/testutils.purpose: Shared, app/common-independent test helpers for the entire openmeter/ tree: Postgres test database provisioning via pgtestdb, async assertion helpers, deterministic name generation, logger factories, and time utilities. Must never import app/common to avoid import cycles.

patterns.name: InitPostgresDB skips when POSTGRES_HOST unset
patterns.description: InitPostgresDB reads os.Getenv("POSTGRES_HOST") and calls t.Skip() if empty. Tests that call this will be silently skipped in environments without Postgres rather than failing.
patterns.example: db := testutils.InitPostgresDB(t) // skips if POSTGRES_HOST not set

patterns.name: TestDB bundles EntDriver + PGDriver + URL
patterns.description: InitPostgresDB returns a *TestDB with both the Ent driver (EntPostgresDriver) and raw pgdriver.Driver for tests that need either. Call db.Close(t) in t.Cleanup to release both.
patterns.example:
db := testutils.InitPostgresDB(t)
t.Cleanup(func() { db.Close(t) })
client := db.EntDriver.Client()

patterns.name: NoopMigrator for schema-less test databases
patterns.description: The default migrator is NoopMigrator (no-op Hash/Migrate/Prepare/Verify). Pass WithMigrator(myMigrator) to apply real schema migrations; otherwise pgtestdb clones a blank template.

patterns.name: NameGenerator produces Key + Name pairs
patterns.description: testutils.NameGenerator.Generate() returns a GeneratedName with a slug Key (lowercase, hyphen-separated) and a Title-case Name. Use Key for database identifiers, Name for display values.

patterns.name: t.Context() throughout — no context.Background()
patterns.description: All helpers use t.Context() (e.g. pgdriver.NewPostgresDriver(t.Context(), ...)) so test cancellation propagates correctly. Never substitute context.Background() in new test helpers.

patterns.name: EventuallyWithTf wraps require.EventuallyWithTf with error capture
patterns.description: Use EventuallyWithTf when the polling function needs to report its last error on final failure. Pass a saveErr closure to record the error; it is surfaced as the EventuallyWithTf format argument.

key_file_guides.role: Core Postgres test provisioning: InitPostgresDB, TestDB struct, option types. The most-imported file in the package.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Importing app/common here — this would create an import cycle. Build Ent clients directly from entdriver.NewEntPostgresDriver(pgDriver.DB()).

key_file_guides.role: Deterministic (per-run) human-readable name generator seeded at package init time.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Using NameGenerator across parallel tests without locking — the underlying generator is not goroutine-safe if multiple goroutines call Generate() concurrently; prefer one call per test.

key_file_guides.role: EventuallyWithTf helper for async assertions that need last-error capture.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Using plain require.Eventually when the polling body produces a meaningful error — EventuallyWithTf surfaces that error on failure.

key_file_guides.role: NewLogger (slog.Default) and NewDiscardLogger (no-op) factories. NewDiscardLogger uses an inline discardHandler to work with Go versions before 1.24.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Using slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stdout, nil)) inline in tests — use NewLogger(t) instead for consistency.

key_file_guides.role: GetRFC3339Time (fatal on parse error) and TimeEqualsApproximately for time comparison with tolerance.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Using time.Parse directly in tests — GetRFC3339Time calls t.Fatalf on error, keeping test code clean.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common or any Wire provider from this package — breaks the test-helper isolation rule
Using context.Background() in helper functions — always use t.Context()
Skipping db.Close(t) in test teardown — leaks Ent and PG connections
Implementing domain-specific test logic here — domain test fixtures belong in openmeter/<domain>/testutils/
Editing pg_driver.go to add real schema migration by default — use WithMigrator option to keep the base helper schema-agnostic

decisions.decision: NoopMigrator as default migrator in InitPostgresDB
decisions.rationale: Most test suites manage their own schema (via Ent auto-migrate or explicit DDL); a no-op default avoids coupling this shared helper to the Atlas migration chain.

decisions.decision: TestDB bundles both EntDriver and PGDriver
decisions.rationale: Some tests need the Ent typed client; others need the raw pgx pool (e.g. for direct SQL assertions). Providing both avoids callers re-constructing drivers from the URL.
